title: NIS2 Cyber Hygiene and Training Compliance

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(g) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(g) (Cyber-hygiene and training) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.10: NIS2 Cyber Hygiene and Training Compliance.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.10' running on sourcetype ms:o365:management and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=training` `sourcetype=lms:completion` (fields course_id, employee_id, completion_date, department) plus HEC JSON from HR; `index=email` `sourcetype=ms:o365:management` for phishing simulation exports from Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 ([Splunkbase 4055](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055)); optional `index=auth` for workforce sign-in volume context. CIM-ready fields after FIELDALIAS: user, action, dest.
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Connect for HTTP Event Collector (built-in), CSV/kvstore lookup `nis2_training_completion`
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_training_completion.csv
| eval days_since_training=round((now()-strptime(completion_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400, 0)
| eval overdue=if(days_since_training > 365 OR isnull(completion_date), 1, 0)
| stats count as total_staff, sum(overdue) as overdue_count, avg(days_since_training) as avg_days_since
| eval compliance_pct=round(100*(total_staff-overdue_count)/total_staff, 1)
| table total_staff, overdue_count, compliance_pct, avg_days_since
description: Measures organization-wide security awareness training freshness by ingesting LMS completion rows — including course identifiers — and flags anyone overdue against a 365-day policy so hygiene evidence can be joined to leadership dashboards.
value: Boards and competent authorities increasingly ask for proof that human-facing controls (training cadence, phishing drill outcomes) keep pace with operational risk; this workflow turns HR and Microsoft 365 telemetry into a repeatable percentage and overdue headcount without waiting for annual PDF exports.
implementation: (1) Publish `nis2_training_completion.csv` nightly via HEC or `outputlookup` from your LMS API worker. (2) Map Microsoft 365 phishing campaigns into `index=email` using TA 4055 MessageTrace modules if simulations land there. (3) Alert when `compliance_pct` < 90. (4) Correlate overdue users with `index=auth` notables via `lookup` on `employee_id`. (5) Archive weekly evidence to `audit_evidence`.
visualization: Single value compliance %, bar chart by department from lookup field, line chart of rolling overdue count, table drilldown to employee_id.
knownFalsePositives: 1) **Microsoft 365 phishing simulations marked as real malware training** causing employees to retake modules unnecessarily. *Distinguish:* `ms:o365:management` payload references an official `campaign_id`. *Suppress:* lookup `nis2_phishing_campaign_allowlist.csv` on `campaign_id`. 2) **Contractors exempt from corporate LMS** appearing overdue forever. *Distinguish:* HR tags `worker_type=contractor` in the CSV. *Suppress:* `where worker_type!="contractor" OR isnotnull(exception_register_id)`. 3) **Employees on parental leave with waived curriculum** still counted in `total_staff`. *Distinguish:* `leave_status=active_leave` in `nis2_training_completion.csv`. *Suppress:* filter `where leave_status!="active_leave"`. 4) **Duplicate LMS rows after HR system migration** double-counting completions. *Distinguish:* identical `completion_date` + `course_id` within one minute. *Suppress:* `| dedup employee_id course_id`. 5) **KnowBe4 or Cornerstone lagging 48h behind** making fresh trainings look overdue. *Distinguish:* `_indextime - _time > 172800` on LMS HEC stream. *Suppress:* time-bound exception register entry valid for the vendor outage window.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• This UC implements NIS2 Art.21(2)(g) — basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training. NIS2 explicitly requires entities to ensure that members of their management bodies attend cybersecurity training (Art.20(2)) and that the entity promotes comparable practices for all employees. This UC monitors training completion rates, phishing simulation results, and policy acknowledgement status to provide evidence that the entity's cyber hygiene programme is effective.
• **Delineation from UC-22.2.41 and UC-22.2.48**: UC-22.2.41 tracks management body (board/executive) cybersecurity training specifically — it focuses on the Art.20(2) requirement for senior leadership. UC-22.2.48 tracks governance approval and management training sign-off. This UC (22.2.10) covers the full employee population — all staff must complete cyber hygiene training and demonstrate competence through phishing simulations.
• **Training data sources**:
  - `index=training sourcetype=lms:completion` — Learning Management System (LMS) completion events. Fields: `user_email`, `course_id`, `course_name`, `completion_date`, `score_pct`, `pass_fail` (pass/fail based on minimum score threshold). Common LMS platforms: Cornerstone OnDemand, SAP SuccessFactors Learning, Workday Learning.
  - `index=training sourcetype=phishing:simulation` — phishing simulation results from KnowBe4, Proofpoint SAT, or similar. Fields: `user_email`, `campaign_id`, `campaign_name`, `sent_date`, `action` (no_action, opened, clicked, reported, submitted_data), `risk_level` (the user's risk classification based on past performance).
  - `nis2_training_requirements.csv` — the training curriculum matrix. Columns: `course_id`, `course_name`, `required_for` (all_employees, it_staff, security_team, management, new_hires), `cadence_months` (how often the training must be repeated — typically 12 for general awareness, 6 for security team), `minimum_score` (pass threshold, e.g., 80).
  - `index=hr sourcetype=hris:user` (optional) — HR system data with `user_email`, `department`, `job_title`, `hire_date`, `termination_date`, `is_active`. Used to determine the population that should have completed training.
• **Phishing simulation programme**: NIS2 recital 89 mentions "basic computer literacy, awareness raising and cyber hygiene" as essential practices. Phishing simulations are the industry standard for measuring whether awareness training translates into behavioural change. Key metrics:
  - **Click rate**: percentage of users who clicked on the simulated phishing link. Target: < 5% for mature programmes.
  - **Report rate**: percentage of users who correctly reported the simulation. Target: > 50%.
  - **Susceptibility**: percentage who submitted data (credentials, PII). Target: < 1%.
• **RBAC**: role `nis2_training_analyst` with `srchIndexesAllowed = training, hr, audit_evidence`. Training data contains employee names and performance — handle as HR-sensitive data.
• **GDPR consideration**: phishing simulation results are personal data (they reveal individual behavioural risk). Ensure the training programme is covered by the organisation's data protection impact assessment (DPIA) and that employees are informed that simulations are conducted.
• **Index retention**: `index=training` ≥ 3 years (training completion evidence). `index=audit_evidence` ≥ 7 years.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**LMS completion data:**
Most LMS platforms provide APIs or SFTP exports. Configure a scripted input or HEC integration:
```ini
[script://./bin/lms_poll.py]
interval = 86400
sourcetype = lms:completion
index = training
```
The script queries the LMS API for completions since the last poll. Alternatively, export CSV from the LMS and place in a monitored directory.

Verify: `index=training sourcetype=lms:completion earliest=-30d | stats count dc(user_email) as users dc(course_id) as courses values(pass_fail) as results`.

**Phishing simulation results:**
For KnowBe4: use the KnowBe4 Reporting API to pull campaign results. The API returns per-user actions (no_action, opened, clicked, reported, submitted_data) for each campaign.
For Proofpoint SAT: use the REST API or syslog integration.

POST to HEC:
```json
{
  "event": {
    "user_email": "john.doe@example.com",
    "campaign_id": "PHI-2026-Q1",
    "action": "clicked",
    "sent_date": "2026-03-15T10:00:00Z"
  },
  "sourcetype": "phishing:simulation",
  "index": "training"
}
```

Verify: `index=training sourcetype=phishing:simulation earliest=-90d | stats count dc(user_email) as users dc(campaign_id) as campaigns values(action) as actions`.

**Training requirements lookup:**
Place `nis2_training_requirements.csv` in `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-nis2-training/lookups/`. Example:
```csv
course_id,course_name,required_for,cadence_months,minimum_score
SEC-101,Cybersecurity Awareness Fundamentals,all_employees,12,80
SEC-201,Advanced Phishing Recognition,it_staff,6,85
SEC-301,Incident Response Procedures,security_team,6,90
SEC-401,NIS2 Obligations for Management,management,12,80
SEC-102,New Hire Security Onboarding,new_hires,0,80
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save as `UC-22.2.10 NIS2 Cyber Hygiene and Training Compliance`.

Understanding this SPL — design decisions:

• **Two main measurement branches**:
  - **Branch 1 — Training completion gap**: cross-references the HR population (active employees) with LMS completion data. For each required course (from the curriculum lookup), identifies employees who: (a) have never completed it, (b) completed it but scored below the minimum, or (c) completed it but the completion has expired (older than `cadence_months`). The output is `completion_pct` per course.
  - **Branch 2 — Phishing simulation risk**: aggregates the latest phishing campaign results to calculate click rate, report rate, and susceptibility by department. Departments with > 10% click rate are flagged as high-risk.

• **`eval training_current=if(completion_epoch > relative_time(now(), "-" . cadence_months . "mon"), 1, 0)`**: determines whether the training is still current. A completion from 13 months ago for a 12-month cadence course is expired.

• **New hire handling**: `required_for=new_hires` with `cadence_months=0` means the training is one-time. Check: `completion_date IS NOT NULL AND completion_date > hire_date`. New hires who haven't completed onboarding training within 30 days are flagged.

Schedule: cron `0 7 1,15 * *` (1st and 15th of each month at 07:00). Training cadences are monthly/quarterly — bi-monthly reporting is appropriate. Trigger: `completion_pct < 90 for any required course OR click_rate > 10%`.

Alert actions: (1) For courses with < 80% completion: email to the HR/Training team with the list of non-compliant users. (2) For departments with > 10% phishing click rate: email to the department head and the security awareness programme manager. (3) Write metrics to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=nis2:training:evidence`.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) **Training gap detection**: identify an active employee who has not completed the mandatory cybersecurity awareness course. Run the search. Confirm the user appears in the gap list.

(b) **Expired training detection**: identify an employee whose last completion was > 12 months ago (for a 12-month cadence course). Run the search. Confirm the employee is counted as non-compliant.

(c) **Phishing click rate**: compare the Splunk-calculated click rate for the latest campaign with the KnowBe4/Proofpoint dashboard. They should agree within 1%.

(d) **New hire compliance**: identify a new hire (hired in the last 30 days). If they haven't completed the onboarding course, they should appear in the results. If they have, they should not.

(e) **Department-level accuracy**: for a known department, manually count employees and completions. Compare with the SPL result.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard (recommended name: `NIS2 Cyber Hygiene and Training`):
• **Row 1 — Training compliance**: Single-value `Overall completion %` (green ≥ 95%). Single-value `Courses below 90%` (red ≥ 1). Single-value `Phishing click rate (latest campaign)` (green < 5%, red > 10%). Single-value `Active employees in scope`.
• **Row 2 — Course-level breakdown**: table of `course_name`, `required_population`, `completed`, `completion_pct`, `average_score`. Colour-code: green ≥ 95%, amber 80-94%, red < 80%.
• **Row 3 — Phishing simulation trends**: `index=training sourcetype=phishing:simulation earliest=-1y | eval month=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m") | stats count(eval(action="clicked")) as clicked count as total by month | eval click_rate=round(100*clicked/total,1)`. Line chart showing click rate trend. Target: declining over time.
• **Row 4 — Department risk heatmap**: table of departments with click rate, report rate, and training completion %. Identify high-risk departments for targeted interventions.

Runbook (owner: Security Awareness Programme Manager / CISO):
1. Bi-monthly: review training gaps. Send reminders to employees with overdue training. Escalate to line managers for employees > 30 days overdue.
2. After each phishing campaign: review results. For users who submitted data (credentials): mandate immediate remedial training and temporary access restrictions. For departments with > 10% click rate: schedule a targeted awareness session.
3. Quarterly: present training metrics to the CISO and management body. Include: completion rates, phishing trends, and department comparisons.
4. Annually: review and update the curriculum. Add new courses for emerging threats (e.g., AI-enabled phishing, deepfake awareness).

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• **LMS `user_email` format mismatch.** The LMS may store emails as `John.Doe@example.com` (mixed case) while the HR system uses `john.doe@example.com`. Fix: normalise: `eval user_email=lower(user_email)` in both sourcetypes.

• **Phishing simulation excluded from training metrics.** Some organisations count phishing simulations as a separate programme from formal training. Fix: keep the two branches separate in the SPL (they already are — Branch 1 for LMS, Branch 2 for phishing). Don't merge them unless the organisation wants a combined metric.

• **HR data not available in Splunk.** Without an HR feed, the denominator (total employees who should complete training) is unknown. Fix: use the LMS's own user population or an Active Directory user export as the population baseline. `| ldapsearch search="(&(objectClass=user)(!(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2)))" attrs="mail,department" | stats dc(mail) as active_users`.

• **Contractors and external users in training data.** Contractors may use different email domains or be excluded from the LMS. Fix: filter by domain or use an HR flag: `where match(user_email, "@example\.com$") OR contractor_training_required="yes"`.

• **GDPR data subject rights.** Employees may request access to or deletion of their training records. Fix: implement a process to export/delete individual training records from the training index. Document this in the DPIA for the security awareness programme.

• **Training completion recorded but not in Splunk.** Some training completions are recorded in paper-based systems or PDFs (e.g., in-person workshops). Fix: create a manual entry CSV `manual_training_completions.csv` and merge with the LMS data using `append`. Include the training type and evidence reference.
grandmaExplanation: We check a simple list that shows who finished this year’s security lessons and who is late, using the same dates the training website stores. That helps leaders prove everyone gets refresher training, not just the tech team.
exclusions: Does not certify that training content meets national requirements, translate legal text, excuse individuals without HR approval, or replace signed attendance sheets where collective bargaining mandates paper records.
evidence: Weekly saved search `UC-22.2.10` output, `nis2_training_completion.csv` version hash recorded in Git, and RFC 3161-signed export blobs referenced by `evidenceSigning` for annual hygiene attestations.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Fixture loads `nis2_training_completion.csv` where thirty percent of rows have `completion_date` older than 400 days; the aggregate search reports `compliance_pct` below seventy and `overdue_count` equal to thirty percent of `total_staff`, confirming the math surfaces stale training.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Fixture refreshes every employee within ninety days and marks contractors with `worker_type=contractor` plus active exceptions in `nis2_phishing_campaign_allowlist.csv`; the overdue drilldown returns zero internal staff after `where worker_type!="contractor"`, demonstrating tuned green-state output.

equipmentModels: Microsoft 365 / Entra ID (user license and training module completion data)
KnowBe4 / Proofpoint Security Awareness Training (phishing simulation results)
Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (HEC for LMS and training data ingest)
